summary: Implemented the footer file info popover feature. Clicking the '3 min read' label in the footer now opens a popover showing the full file path and frontmatter fields for the open document. Key changes: (1) Added onFrontmatterChange callback to DocumentViewer, mirroring the existing onReadingTimeChange pattern; (2) Added frontmatter state to App, passed filePath and frontmatter down to FooterBar; (3) Created FileInfoPopoverContent component with Path and Frontmatter sections, doc_id exclusion, array comma-joining, and selectable text; (4) Wrapped FooterBar's reading-time span in a Radix UI Popover trigger (side=top, sideOffset=24); (5) Added 10 unit tests for FileInfoPopoverContent and 5 new FooterBar tests. All 782 tests pass.
testing_instructions:
Branch: spec/df68fe63-7b71-49d9-ba1c-c526ed46c461
Setup: npm install && npm run build (or just run the dev server with: npm run tauri dev)
Test: 1. Open Episteme and select any markdown document from the sidebar. The footer center zone should show 'X min read' as a clickable label. 2. Click the reading-time label — a popover should appear above the footer showing a 'Path' section with the full file path (selectable text) and, if the document has frontmatter, a 'Frontmatter' section with key/value pairs (doc_id excluded). 3. Click outside the popover or press Escape to dismiss it. 4. Open a document with no frontmatter — the popover should show only the Path section. 5. Open a document with frontmatter that includes an array field — the value should render as comma-separated text. 6. Switch to a document with no content open (welcome screen) — the footer center zone should be empty with no clickable label. Run unit tests: npx vitest run
